'''Liligiifo Sao''' (born 11 October 1992) is a [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} and {{rlp|lf}} for [[Hull F.C.]] in the [[Super League]].

Sao previously played for the [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles]] and the [[New Zealand Warriors]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]].

==Background==
Sao was born in [[Auckland, New Zealand]] and is of [[Samoan people|Samoan]] descent.

He played his junior football for the [[Manurewa Marlins]] in the [[Auckland Rugby League]] competition.<ref name=Warriors2016a/>

==Playing career==
===Club career===
====Early career====
He was then signed by the [[New Zealand Warriors]], playing in the [[2012 NRL Under-20s season|2012 Toyota Cup]] for the Junior Warriors.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/982/ligi-sao-joins-fc |title=Ligi Sao Joins FC |publisher=Betfred Super League |date=15 August 2019 |access-date=9 July 2022}}</ref>

====Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles====
In 2013, Sao signed with the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]]. Sao played in both the [[2013 NRL Under-20s season|2013 Holden Cup]] and the 2013 [[NSW Cup]] before making his first grade debut on 15 July 2013 against the [[North Queensland Cowboys]].<ref name=rlp/>

====New Zealand Warriors====
On 22 July 2015, three days before they were due to play Manly in Auckland, the Warriors announced that they had signed Sao for the 2016 and 2017 seasons.<ref name=Warriors2016a>{{cite web|author= |url=http://www.warriors.kiwi/news/2015/07/22/sao_to_rejoin_vodafo.html |title=PLAYER SIGNING | Sao coming home - Warriors |website=Warriors.kiwi |date= 22 July 2015|accessdate=2017-04-04}}</ref>

====Hull F.C.====
He joined [[Super League]] side [[Hull F.C.]] for the 2020 season. Sao played 16 games for Hull F.C. in the [[2020 Super League season]] including the club's semi-final defeat against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/wigan-warriors-hull-fc-highlights-4716172#964303/|title=Wigan Warriors v Hull FC highlights as FC struggle to find play-off form in one-sided defeat|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk|date=19 November 2020 }}</ref>
Sao played 21 games for Hull F.C. in the [[2021 Super League season]] which saw the club finish 8th on the table and miss the playoffs. In round 20 of the 2022 Super League season, Sao was sent off for a dangerous high tackle in Hull FC's 46-18 loss against [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2022-07-22 |title=Super League: Derrell Olpherts stars as ruthless Castleford Tigers stun Hull FC 46-18 |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12657086/super-league-derrell-olpherts-stars-as-ruthless-castleford-tigers-stun-hull-fc-46-18 |access-date=2022-07-23 |website=Sky Sports}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2022-07-22 |title=Super League: Hull FC 18-46 Castleford - Lee Radford's Tigers come from behind to win |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/62238358 |access-date=2022-07-23 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>
Sao played 17 games for Hull F.C. for in the [[Super League XXVIII]] season as the club finished 10th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc-high-lows-mid-8799429/|title=Hull FC highs and lows as mid-season resurgence not enough to prevent drop-off|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk|access-date=4 October 2023|archive-date=4 October 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231004073738/https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc-high-lows-mid-8799429|url-status=dead}}</ref>
In round 1 of the 2024 Super League season, Sao was sent to the sin bin during the clubs 22-0 loss to arch-rivals Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/hull-fc-new-signing-franklin-pele-sent-off-after-horror-swinging-arm-on-elliot-minchella/|title=Hull FC new signing Franklin Pele sent off after horror swinging arm on Elliot Minchella|website=www.totalrl.com|date=15 February 2024 }}</ref>
Sao was limited to just two games for Hull F.C. in the 2025 Super League season as the club finished 7th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/grading-each-of-super-leagues-bottom-six-in-2025-with-warrington-getting-the-lowest-marks/|title=Grading each of Super League’s bottom six in 2025 with Warrington getting the lowest marks|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}}</ref>

== International career ==
In October 2022, Sao was named in the [[2021 Men's Rugby League World Cup squads#Samoa|Samoa squad]] for the [[2021 Rugby League World Cup – Men's tournament|2021 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref>[https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/rugby-league-world-cup-2021-squad-lists/ Full list of every squad at the Rugby League World Cup 2021]</ref>
